message: fix(wrappers): harden stale home-adjacent state dispatch

files.patch:
@@ -88,6 +88,45 @@ read_target_home_from_state_file() {
     return 1
 }
 
+find_acfs_bin_from_state_file() {
+    local state_file="$1"
+    local state_home=""
+    local target_home=""
+    local target_user=""
+    local candidate_bin=""
+
+    [[ -f "$state_file" ]] || return 1
+
+    if [[ "$state_file" == */.acfs/state.json ]]; then
+        state_home="${state_file%/.acfs/state.json}"
+        state_home="${state_home%/}"
+        if [[ -n "$state_home" ]] && [[ "$state_home" == /* ]] && [[ "$state_home" != "/" ]]; then
+            candidate_bin="$(find_acfs_bin_for_home "$state_home" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+            if [[ -n "$candidate_bin" ]]; then
+                printf '%s\n' "$candidate_bin"
+                return 0
+            fi
+
+            # For home-adjacent state files, the path is a stronger signal than
+            # any embedded target_user copied from another install.
+            return 1
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    target_home="$(read_target_home_from_state_file "$state_file" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    if [[ -n "$target_home" ]]; then
+        candidate_bin="$(find_acfs_bin_for_home "$target_home" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+        if [[ -n "$candidate_bin" ]]; then
+            printf '%s\n' "$candidate_bin"
+            return 0
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    target_user="$(read_target_user_from_state_file "$state_file" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    [[ -n "$target_user" ]] || return 1
+    find_acfs_bin "$target_user"
+}
+
 find_acfs_bin_for_home() {
     local user_home="$1"
 
@@ -173,6 +212,18 @@ read_target_user_from_state_hint() {
         fi
     fi
 
+    state_home="$(read_target_home_from_state_file "$state_file" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    if [[ -n "$state_home" ]] && [[ "$state_home" == /* ]] && [[ "$state_home" != "/" ]]; then
+        candidate_user="$(read_user_for_home "$state_home" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+        if [[ -z "$candidate_user" ]]; then
+            candidate_user="$(read_target_user_from_state_file "$state_file" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+        fi
+        if [[ -n "$candidate_user" ]] && find_acfs_bin_for_home "$state_home" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+            printf '%s\n' "$candidate_user"
+            return 0
+        fi
+    fi
+
     candidate_user="$(read_target_user_from_state_file "$state_file" 2>/dev/null || true)"
     if [[ -n "$candidate_user" ]] && find_acfs_bin "$candidate_user" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
         printf '%s\n' "$candidate_user"

+        candidate_bin="$(find_acfs_bin_from_state_file "$ACFS_SYSTEM_STATE_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+        [[ -n "$candidate_bin" ]] && acfs_bin="$candidate_bin"
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -z "$acfs_bin" ]]; then
+        acfs_bin="$(find_acfs_bin "$target_user" || true)"
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -z "$acfs_bin" ]]; then
         echo "Error: ACFS not found for user '$target_user'" >&2
 
         local target_home

@@ -82,6 +82,45 @@ read_target_home_from_state_file() {
   return 1
 }
 
+find_update_script_for_state_file() {
+  local state_file="$1"
+  local state_home=""
+  local target_home=""
+  local target_user=""
+  local candidate_script=""
+
+  [[ -f "$state_file" ]] || return 1
+
+  if [[ "$state_file" == */.acfs/state.json ]]; then
+    state_home="${state_file%/.acfs/state.json}"
+    state_home="${state_home%/}"
+    if [[ -n "$state_home" ]] && [[ "$state_home" == /* ]] && [[ "$state_home" != "/" ]]; then
+      candidate_script="$(find_update_script_for_home "$state_home" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+      if [[ -n "$candidate_script" ]]; then
+        printf '%s\n' "$candidate_script"
+        return 0
+      fi
+
+      # For home-adjacent state files, the path is a stronger signal than any
+      # embedded target_user copied from another install.
+      return 1
+    fi
+  fi
+
+  target_home="$(read_target_home_from_state_file "$state_file" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+  if [[ -n "$target_home" ]]; then
+    candidate_script="$(find_update_script_for_home "$target_home" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    if [[ -n "$candidate_script" ]]; then
+      printf '%s\n' "$candidate_script"
+      return 0
+    fi
+  fi
+
+  target_user="$(read_target_user_from_state_file "$state_file" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+  [[ -n "$target_user" ]] || return 1
+  find_update_script_for_user "$target_user"
+}
+
 find_wrapper_installed_home() {
   local candidate_home=""
 
@@ -149,6 +188,51 @@ read_user_for_home() {
   return 1
 }
 
+read_target_user_from_state_hint() {
+  local state_file="$1"
+  local candidate_user=""
+  local state_home=""
+  local target_home=""
+
+  [[ -f "$state_file" ]] || return 1
+
+  if [[ "$state_file" == */.acfs/state.json ]]; then
+    state_home="${state_file%/.acfs/state.json}"
+    state_home="${state_home%/}"
+    if [[ -n "$state_home" ]] && [[ "$state_home" == /* ]] && [[ "$state_home" != "/" ]]; then
+      candidate_user="$(read_user_for_home "$state_home" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+      if [[ -n "$candidate_user" ]] && find_update_script_for_home "$state_home" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        printf '%s\n' "$candidate_user"
+        return 0
+      fi
+
+      # For home-adjacent state files, refuse to trust mismatched target_user
+      # values from stale copies. The path itself is the stronger signal.
+      return 1
+    fi
+  fi
+
+  target_home="$(read_target_home_from_state_file "$state_file" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+  if [[ -n "$target_home" ]] && find_update_script_for_home "$target_home" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+    candidate_user="$(read_user_for_home "$target_home" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    if [[ -z "$candidate_user" ]]; then
+      candidate_user="$(read_target_user_from_state_file "$state_file" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    fi
+    if [[ -n "$candidate_user" ]]; then
+      printf '%s\n' "$candidate_user"
+      return 0
+    fi
+  fi
+
+  candidate_user="$(read_target_user_from_state_file "$state_file" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+  if [[ -n "$candidate_user" ]] && user_has_installed_update_script "$candidate_user"; then
+    printf '%s\n' "$candidate_user"
+    return 0
+  fi
+
+  return 1
+}
+
 find_update_script_for_home() {
   local user_home="$1"

   if [[ -n "${ACFS_HOME:-}" ]]; then

-    if [[ -n "$candidate_user" ]] && user_has_installed_update_script "$candidate_user"; then
+    candidate_user="$(read_target_user_from_state_hint "${ACFS_HOME}/state.json" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    if [[ -n "$candidate_user" ]]; then
       printf '%s\n' "$candidate_user"
       return 0
     fi
   fi
 
-  candidate_user="$(read_target_user_from_state_file "$HOME/.acfs/state.json" 2>/dev/null || true)"
-  if [[ -n "$candidate_user" ]] && user_has_installed_update_script "$candidate_user"; then

     printf '%s\n' "$candidate_user"

     [[ "$candidate_home" == /* ]] || continue
     state_candidate="$candidate_home/.acfs/state.json"
-    candidate_user="$(read_target_user_from_state_file "$state_candidate" 2>/dev/null || true)"

     printf '%s\n' "$candidate_user"
     return 0
   fi

   repo_update_script="$(find_repo_local_update_script || true)"
   wrapper_home="$(find_wrapper_installed_home || true)"
@@ -337,7 +422,29 @@ main() {
     if [[ -z "$target_user" ]]; then
       target_user="$(read_user_for_home "$wrapper_home" || true)"
     fi
-  elif [[ -n "$target_user" ]]; then
+  fi

+  if [[ -z "$update_script" ]] && [[ -n "${ACFS_STATE_FILE:-}" ]]; then

+    [[ -n "$candidate_script" ]] && update_script="$candidate_script"

+
+  if [[ -z "$update_script" ]]; then
+    candidate_script="$(find_update_script_for_state_file "$HOME/.acfs/state.json" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    [[ -n "$candidate_script" ]] && update_script="$candidate_script"
+  fi
+
+  if [[ -z "$update_script" ]]; then
+    candidate_script="$(find_update_script_for_state_file "$ACFS_SYSTEM_STATE_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    [[ -n "$candidate_script" ]] && update_script="$candidate_script"
+  fi
+
+  if [[ -z "$update_script" ]] && [[ -n "$target_user" ]]; then
     update_script="$(find_update_script_for_user "$target_user" || true)"
   fi

@@ -1155,6 +1155,217 @@ test_doctor_dispatches_installed_layout_under_root_home() {
     cleanup_mock_env
 }
 
+test_acfs_update_wrapper_uses_system_state_target_home_when_getent_unavailable() {
+    setup_system_state_target_home_env
+
+    mkdir -p "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.acfs/scripts/lib" "$TEST_HOME/probe"
+    printf '#!/usr/bin/env bash\n' > "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.acfs/scripts/lib/update.sh"
+    chmod +x "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.acfs/scripts/lib/update.sh"
+    cp "$REPO_ROOT/scripts/acfs-update" "$TEST_HOME/probe/acfs-update"
+    chmod +x "$TEST_HOME/probe/acfs-update"
+
+    cat > "$TEST_FAKE_BIN/sudo" <<'EOF'
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+printf 'sudo-argv=%s\n' "$*"
+EOF
+    cat > "$TEST_FAKE_BIN/stat" <<EOF
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+if [[ "\$1" == "-c" ]] && [[ "\$2" == "%U" ]] && [[ "\$3" == "$TEST_TARGET_HOME" ]]; then
+    printf 'tester\n'
+    exit 0
+fi
+exec /usr/bin/stat "\$@"
+EOF
+    chmod +x "$TEST_FAKE_BIN/sudo" "$TEST_FAKE_BIN/stat"
+
+    local output=""
+    output=$(HOME="$TEST_ROOT_HOME" PATH="$TEST_FAKE_BIN:/usr/bin:/bin" \
+        ACFS_SYSTEM_STATE_FILE="$TEST_SYSTEM_STATE_FILE" \
+        bash "$TEST_HOME/probe/acfs-update" --help 2>&1)
+
+    if [[ "$output" == *"ACFS_SYSTEM_STATE_FILE=$TEST_SYSTEM_STATE_FILE"* ]] \
+        && [[ "$output" == *"$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.acfs/scripts/lib/update.sh --help"* ]] \
+        && [[ "$output" == *"-u tester -H"* ]]; then
+        harness_pass "acfs-update wrapper uses system-state target_home when getent is unavailable"
+    else
+        harness_fail "acfs-update wrapper uses system-state target_home when getent is unavailable" "$output"
+    fi
+
+    cleanup_mock_env
+}
+
+test_acfs_update_wrapper_ignores_stale_home_adjacent_target_user() {
+    setup_mock_env
+
+    TEST_ROOT_HOME="$TEST_HOME/root-home"
+    TEST_TARGET_HOME="$TEST_HOME/custom-home"
+    TEST_FAKE_BIN="$TEST_HOME/fake-bin"
+    local other_home="$TEST_HOME/other-home"
+
+    mkdir -p \
+        "$TEST_ROOT_HOME" \
+        "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.acfs/scripts/lib" \
+        "$other_home/.acfs/scripts/lib" \
+        "$TEST_HOME/probe" \
+        "$TEST_FAKE_BIN"
+
+    cat > "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.acfs/state.json" <<'JSON'
+{
+  "target_user": "otheruser"
+}
+JSON
+    printf '#!/usr/bin/env bash\n' > "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.acfs/scripts/lib/update.sh"
+    printf '#!/usr/bin/env bash\n' > "$other_home/.acfs/scripts/lib/update.sh"
+    chmod +x "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.acfs/scripts/lib/update.sh" "$other_home/.acfs/scripts/lib/update.sh"
+    cp "$REPO_ROOT/scripts/acfs-update" "$TEST_HOME/probe/acfs-update"
+    chmod +x "$TEST_HOME/probe/acfs-update"
+
+    cat > "$TEST_FAKE_BIN/getent" <<EOF
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+if [[ "\$1" == "passwd" ]] && [[ -z "\${2:-}" ]]; then
+    printf 'tester:x:1000:1000::%s:/bin/bash\n' "$TEST_TARGET_HOME"
+    printf 'otheruser:x:1001:1001::%s:/bin/bash\n' "$other_home"
+    exit 0
+fi
+if [[ "\$1" == "passwd" ]] && [[ "\$2" == "tester" ]]; then
+    printf 'tester:x:1000:1000::%s:/bin/bash\n' "$TEST_TARGET_HOME"
+    exit 0
+fi
+if [[ "\$1" == "passwd" ]] && [[ "\$2" == "otheruser" ]]; then
+    printf 'otheruser:x:1001:1001::%s:/bin/bash\n' "$other_home"
+    exit 0
+fi
+exit 2
+EOF
+    cat > "$TEST_FAKE_BIN/sudo" <<'EOF'
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+printf 'sudo-argv=%s\n' "$*"
+EOF
+    chmod +x "$TEST_FAKE_BIN/getent" "$TEST_FAKE_BIN/sudo"
+
+    local output=""
+    output=$(HOME="$TEST_ROOT_HOME" PATH="$TEST_FAKE_BIN:/usr/bin:/bin" \
+        ACFS_STATE_FILE="$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.acfs/state.json" \
+        bash "$TEST_HOME/probe/acfs-update" --help 2>&1)
+
+    if [[ "$output" == *"$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.acfs/scripts/lib/update.sh --help"* ]] \
+        && [[ "$output" == *"-u tester -H"* ]] \
+        && [[ "$output" != *"$other_home/.acfs/scripts/lib/update.sh"* ]] \
+        && [[ "$output" != *"-u otheruser -H"* ]]; then
+        harness_pass "acfs-update wrapper ignores stale home-adjacent target_user"
+    else
+        harness_fail "acfs-update wrapper ignores stale home-adjacent target_user" "$output"
+    fi
+
+    cleanup_mock_env
+}
+
+test_acfs_global_wrapper_uses_system_state_target_home_when_getent_unavailable() {
+    setup_system_state_target_home_env
+
+    mkdir -p "$TEST_HOME/probe"
+    printf '#!/usr/bin/env bash\n' > "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.local/bin/acfs"
+    chmod +x "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.local/bin/acfs"
+    cp "$REPO_ROOT/scripts/acfs-global" "$TEST_HOME/probe/acfs"
+    chmod +x "$TEST_HOME/probe/acfs"
+
+    cat > "$TEST_FAKE_BIN/sudo" <<'EOF'
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+printf 'sudo-argv=%s\n' "$*"
+EOF
+    cat > "$TEST_FAKE_BIN/stat" <<EOF
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+if [[ "\$1" == "-c" ]] && [[ "\$2" == "%U" ]] && [[ "\$3" == "$TEST_TARGET_HOME" ]]; then
+    printf 'tester\n'
+    exit 0
+fi
+exec /usr/bin/stat "\$@"
+EOF
+    chmod +x "$TEST_FAKE_BIN/sudo" "$TEST_FAKE_BIN/stat"
+
+    local output=""
+    output=$(HOME="$TEST_ROOT_HOME" PATH="$TEST_FAKE_BIN:/usr/bin:/bin" \
+        ACFS_SYSTEM_STATE_FILE="$TEST_SYSTEM_STATE_FILE" \
+        bash "$TEST_HOME/probe/acfs" version 2>&1)
+
+    if [[ "$output" == *"ACFS_SYSTEM_STATE_FILE=$TEST_SYSTEM_STATE_FILE"* ]] \
+        && [[ "$output" == *"$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.local/bin/acfs version"* ]] \
+        && [[ "$output" == *"-u tester -H"* ]]; then
+        harness_pass "global acfs wrapper uses system-state target_home when getent is unavailable"
+    else
+        harness_fail "global acfs wrapper uses system-state target_home when getent is unavailable" "$output"
+    fi
+
+    cleanup_mock_env
+}
+
+test_acfs_global_wrapper_ignores_stale_home_adjacent_target_user() {
+    setup_mock_env
+
+    TEST_ROOT_HOME="$TEST_HOME/root-home"
+    TEST_TARGET_HOME="$TEST_HOME/custom-home"
+    TEST_FAKE_BIN="$TEST_HOME/fake-bin"
+    local other_home="$TEST_HOME/other-home"
+
+    mkdir -p \
+        "$TEST_ROOT_HOME" \
+        "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.acfs" \
+        "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.local/bin" \
+        "$other_home/.local/bin" \
+        "$TEST_HOME/probe" \
+        "$TEST_FAKE_BIN"
+
+    cat > "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.acfs/state.json" <<'JSON'
+{
+  "target_user": "otheruser"
+}
+JSON
+    printf '#!/usr/bin/env bash\n' > "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.local/bin/acfs"
+    printf '#!/usr/bin/env bash\n' > "$other_home/.local/bin/acfs"
+    chmod +x "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.local/bin/acfs" "$other_home/.local/bin/acfs"
+    cp "$REPO_ROOT/scripts/acfs-global" "$TEST_HOME/probe/acfs"
+    chmod +x "$TEST_HOME/probe/acfs"
+
+    cat > "$TEST_FAKE_BIN/getent" <<EOF
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+if [[ "\$1" == "passwd" ]] && [[ -z "\${2:-}" ]]; then
+    printf 'tester:x:1000:1000::%s:/bin/bash\n' "$TEST_TARGET_HOME"
+    printf 'otheruser:x:1001:1001::%s:/bin/bash\n' "$other_home"
+    exit 0
+fi
+if [[ "\$1" == "passwd" ]] && [[ "\$2" == "tester" ]]; then
+    printf 'tester:x:1000:1000::%s:/bin/bash\n' "$TEST_TARGET_HOME"
+    exit 0
+fi
+if [[ "\$1" == "passwd" ]] && [[ "\$2" == "otheruser" ]]; then
+    printf 'otheruser:x:1001:1001::%s:/bin/bash\n' "$other_home"
+    exit 0
+fi
+exit 2
+EOF
+    cat > "$TEST_FAKE_BIN/sudo" <<'EOF'
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+printf 'sudo-argv=%s\n' "$*"
+EOF
+    chmod +x "$TEST_FAKE_BIN/getent" "$TEST_FAKE_BIN/sudo"
+
+    local output=""
+    output=$(HOME="$TEST_ROOT_HOME" PATH="$TEST_FAKE_BIN:/usr/bin:/bin" \
+        ACFS_STATE_FILE="$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.acfs/state.json" \
+        bash "$TEST_HOME/probe/acfs" version 2>&1)
+
+    if [[ "$output" == *"$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.local/bin/acfs version"* ]] \
+        && [[ "$output" == *"-u tester -H"* ]] \
+        && [[ "$output" != *"$other_home/.local/bin/acfs"* ]] \
+        && [[ "$output" != *"-u otheruser -H"* ]]; then
+        harness_pass "global acfs wrapper ignores stale home-adjacent target_user"
+    else
+        harness_fail "global acfs wrapper ignores stale home-adjacent target_user" "$output"
+    fi
+
+    cleanup_mock_env
+}
+
 test_doctor_agent_checks_use_target_context_under_root_home() {
     setup_installed_layout_env

     harness_section "Entrypoint Dispatch"

+    test_acfs_global_wrapper_uses_system_state_target_home_when_getent_unavailable || true

     test_doctor_agent_checks_use_target_context_under_root_home || true
     test_doctor_deep_agent_auth_uses_target_context_under_root_home || true
     test_doctor_deep_optional_probes_use_target_home_under_root_home || true
